title: Clockify Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Clockify** is a time-tracking SaaS exposing a REST API at `https://api.clockify.me/api/v1` plus a separate Reports (analytics) API at `https://reports.api.clockify.me/v1`. This connector — `ClockifyConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "clockify"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Clockify workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| User | `/user` | Identity probe — used by `health_check()` |
| Workspaces | `/workspaces` | List every workspace the user belongs to |
| Projects | `/workspaces/{wid}/projects` | List, get, create projects |
| Tasks | `/workspaces/{wid}/projects/{pid}/tasks` | List tasks per project |
| Clients | `/workspaces/{wid}/clients` | List + create clients |
| Tags | `/workspaces/{wid}/tags` | List tags |
| Users | `/workspaces/{wid}/users` | List workspace members |
| Time entries | `/workspaces/{wid}/time-entries` + `/workspaces/{wid}/user/{uid}/time-entries` | Full CRUD + start/stop running timer |
| Reports | `/workspaces/{wid}/reports/summary` (reports host) | Summary report |

The connector normalises time entries into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::ClockifyHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Clockify API key

1. Sign in at https://app.clockify.me.
2. Click your avatar (top-right) → **Profile Settings**.
3. Scroll to the **API** section.
4. Click **Generate** and copy the key. Treat it like a password — it grants full read/write access to every workspace the user belongs to.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Clockify**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 4 |
| `default_workspace_id` (text, optional) | Workspace UUID — used by `sync()` when blank caller-side |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.clockify.me/api/v1` (self-hosted only) |
| `reports_base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://reports.api.clockify.me/v1` (self-hosted only) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` is present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `ClockifyHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which performs `GET /user` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 / 403 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Clockify REST API uses **server-to-server API key authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth, no token refresh, no expiry — the key lives until explicitly rotated.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the **`X-Api-Key`** header. Clockify does NOT accept an `Authorization` header — sending one is a no-op and will fail auth.
- `Content-Type: application/json` and `Accept: application/json` are sent on every request.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Initialises `ClockifyHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Clockify endpoint. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | Synthesises a `TokenInfo(token_type="ApiKey", access_token=api_key)` so the SDK session bookkeeping stays uniform; no exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /user` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | The api_key is stored via `save_config()` at install time; `set_token()` records a synthesised `TokenInfo` for SDK consistency. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
X-Api-Key:    <api_key>
Content-Type: application/json
Accept:       application/json
```

Reports API calls go to a different host (`https://reports.api.clockify.me/v1`) but use the same `X-Api-Key` header.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `X-Api-Key` header value |
| `default_workspace_id` | text | no | `self.default_workspace_id` | Default workspace for `sync()` |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.clockify.me/api/v1`) | Override for self-hosted Clockify |
| `reports_base_url` | text | no | `self.reports_base_url` (defaults to `https://reports.api.clockify.me/v1`) | Override for self-hosted Reports API |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_API_BASE     = "https://api.clockify.me/api/v1"
_DEFAULT_REPORTS_BASE = "https://reports.api.clockify.me/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `ClockifyConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::ClockifyHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; initialise `ClockifyHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code='', state=None) -> TokenInfo`
Synthesise a `TokenInfo(token_type="ApiKey")` — no exchange. Stored via `set_token()` for SDK bookkeeping.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /user` — minimal probe. Classifies failures via the exception hierarchy:
  * `ClockifyAuthError` → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`
  * `ClockifyNetworkError` → `OFFLINE + CONNECTED`
  * other `ClockifyError` → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED`

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
For the authenticated user, walks time entries from the default workspace (or the first workspace returned by `/workspaces` when blank), normalises each entry → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult(status, documents_synced, documents_failed, documents_found, message)`.

children.title: Identity & Workspaces
children.content:
### `async get_current_user() -> Dict`
`GET /user`.

### `async list_workspaces() -> List[Dict]`
`GET /workspaces`.

### `async list_users(workspace_id, *, page=1, page_size=50, status=None) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /workspaces/{wid}/users` — list workspace members. Pass `status='ACTIVE'` / `'PENDING'` / `'INACTIVE'` to filter.

children.title: Projects & Tasks
children.content:
### `async list_projects(workspace_id, *, archived=False, name=None, page=1, page_size=50) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /workspaces/{wid}/projects`.

### `async get_project(workspace_id, project_id) -> Dict`
`GET /workspaces/{wid}/projects/{pid}`.

### `async create_project(workspace_id, name, *, client_id=None, color=None, is_public=False, billable=False, hourly_rate=None) -> Dict`
`POST /workspaces/{wid}/projects` — body builds camelCase (`isPublic`, `clientId`, `hourlyRate`).

### `async list_tasks(workspace_id, project_id, *, page=1, page_size=50, status='ACTIVE') -> List[Dict]`
`GET /workspaces/{wid}/projects/{pid}/tasks`.

children.title: Time Entries — CRUD + Timer
children.content:
### `async list_time_entries(workspace_id, user_id, *, page=1, page_size=50, start=None, end=None, project=None) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /workspaces/{wid}/user/{uid}/time-entries` — ISO-8601 `start` / `end` filters supported.

### `async get_time_entry(workspace_id, entry_id) -> Dict`
`GET /workspaces/{wid}/time-entries/{eid}`.

### `async create_time_entry(workspace_id, start, *, end=None, project_id=None, task_id=None, description='', tag_ids=None, billable=False) -> Dict`
`POST /workspaces/{wid}/time-entries`. Omit `end` for a running timer (prefer `start_time_entry` for clarity).

### `async start_time_entry(workspace_id, *, start=None, project_id=None, task_id=None, description='', tag_ids=None, billable=False) -> Dict`
Start a running timer. `start` defaults to current UTC if omitted. The payload deliberately omits the `end` field.

### `async stop_time_entry(workspace_id, user_id, *, end=None) -> Dict`
`PATCH /workspaces/{wid}/user/{uid}/time-entries` with body `{end}`. `end` defaults to current UTC if omitted.

### `async update_time_entry(workspace_id, entry_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PUT /workspaces/{wid}/time-entries/{eid}` — generic patch body.

### `async delete_time_entry(workspace_id, entry_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /workspaces/{wid}/time-entries/{eid}` — 204 returns `{}`.

children.title: Tags & Clients
children.content:
### `async list_tags(workspace_id, *, archived=False, page=1) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /workspaces/{wid}/tags`.

### `async list_clients(workspace_id, *, archived=False, page=1, page_size=50) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /workspaces/{wid}/clients`.

### `async create_client(workspace_id, name, *, address=None, email=None) -> Dict`
`POST /workspaces/{wid}/clients`.

children.title: Reports
children.content:
### `async summary_report(workspace_id, date_range_start, date_range_end, *, summary_filter=None, users=None, projects=None) -> Dict`
`POST {reports_base}/workspaces/{wid}/reports/summary` — **different host** (`reports.api.clockify.me`). The connector auto-supplies a minimal `summaryFilter` (`{groups: ['PROJECT']}`) when the caller omits it, because Clockify rejects requests without one.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
ClockifyError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── ClockifyAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── ClockifyNotFound                       # 404
├── ClockifyRateLimitError                 # 429
└── ClockifyNetworkError                   # transport / 5xx (transport-side)
```

### Retry behaviour

Layered two-step retry:

1. **Inner** (`client/http_client.py::_request`) — retries 429 + 5xx + `httpx.HTTPError` up to `_INNER_RETRIES=2` with exponential backoff + jitter (`0.5 * 2 ** attempt + random[0, 0.25]`). Honours `Retry-After` header when present.
2. **Outer** (`helpers/utils.py::with_retry`) — wraps each connector call up to `max_retries=3` with `BACKOFF_FACTOR=2.0` and `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S=32.0`. Catches `ClockifyRateLimitError` + `ClockifyNetworkError` only.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches the typed exceptions and maps:

```
ClockifyAuthError    → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
ClockifyNetworkError → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
ClockifyError        → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

For mutating calls (`create_project`, `update_time_entry`, etc.), the connector lets the exception propagate so the caller (gateway / SDK) can map it to an HTTP status.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the key in ACP. Clockify shows the key only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — API key invalid or revoked | Rotate the key in Clockify → Profile Settings → API. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — key valid but lacks workspace permissions | Confirm the user owning the key has access to the workspaces you are reading. |
| `clockify.transient_retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Clockify per-second / per-minute cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff + jitter. Lower request fan-out or batch fewer rows per cycle. |
| 404 on `summary_report` | Workspace ID wrong OR Reports API host blocked | Confirm `workspace_id` matches `list_workspaces()` output. Reports API uses a different host — check egress allowlist. |
| `ClockifyError: summaryFilter required` | Caller passed `summary_filter=None` AND removed default | The connector auto-supplies `{groups: ['PROJECT']}` when blank; do not pass `summary_filter={}` (empty dict overrides the default). |
| `start_time_entry` succeeds but Clockify UI still shows old timer | A running timer already exists | Call `stop_time_entry(workspace_id, user_id)` first — Clockify allows only one running timer per user. |
| `Authorization` header leaks | Caller code injects `Authorization` thinking Clockify uses it | Clockify ignores `Authorization` entirely — it reads `X-Api-Key`. Remove the wrong header. |
| Time entries missing in `sync()` | `since` filter excludes them OR they belong to a different user | `sync()` only walks the *authenticated* user's entries. To sync teammates, use `list_users()` + `list_time_entries(user_id=…)` directly. |
